cases.prompt: Create a new Rust CLI tool that parses web server access logs and prints per-endpoint latency statistics as text or JSON.
expected_decisions: Binary crate starts with a thin main.rs plus lib.rs and domain modules, not a single-file implementation
Test placement follows Rust conventions: unit tests beside modules, integration tests in tests/
expected_evidence: Layout decisions recorded as file-to-role mapping
cases.notes: Greenfield project creation is generative layout work; a host CLI is not embedded work.

cases.prompt: src/main.rs has grown to 700 lines and about half of it is #[cfg(test)] test code. Clean this up.
expected_decisions: Domain logic moves from main.rs into lib.rs modules
Inline tests move to sibling test modules or tests/ integration tests
cases.notes: Structure budget breach on an entrypoint; design-balance names the split units.

cases.prompt: Where should the integration tests for this crate live, and which tests can stay inside the module files?
expected_decisions: Unit tests stay in #[cfg(test)] modules within budget; integration tests go to tests/ against the public API
cases.notes: Pure placement question routes to the layout skill.

cases.prompt: Rename the local variable `tmp` to `parsed_entry` inside one function in src/parser.rs.
cases.notes: Tiny local edit; structure watch still runs inside dev-workflow but the layout skill itself should not load.

cases.prompt: Compare SQLite and Postgres as the storage backend given our latency and operational-cost targets.
cases.notes: Cross-boundary option comparison is architecture analysis, not physical layout.
